name: agent-ready-mcp
description: Scan any URL for AI agent readability against the Vercel Agent Readability Spec, the llmstxt.org standard, and agent-protocol manifests (MCP server cards, A2A, agents.json, agent-permissions.json, UCP, x402, NLWeb). 68 checks with per-check fix guidance.
long_description: Agent Ready MCP runs the agent-ready.dev scanner against any public URL and returns a structured agent-readability report: a 0–100 Vercel Agent Readability score, a 0–100 llms.txt sub-score, and per-check findings with concrete remediation hints. The scanner runs 68 checks across four spec families — 38 against the Vercel Agent Readability Spec (15 site-wide plus 23 per-page covering meta tags, JSON-LD, headings, markdown mirrors, content negotiation, code-block language tags, JS-rendering dependency), 10 against the llmstxt.org specification, and 20 against agent protocols (MCP Server Cards per SEP-1649 / RFC 9728, A2A Agent Cards, Wildcard agents.json, agent-permissions.json, UCP, x402, NLWeb, API Catalog per RFC 9727, Web Bot Auth, Agent Skills Discovery, agent-driven UI / A2UI, MPP and AP2 payments, plus a content-integrity cloaking check). Three discovery prompts wire end-to-end workflows: 'scan' (URL → summary), 'interpret_scan' (id → plain-English findings), 'remediation_plan' (id → prioritised fix-it doc). Pairs with a code-editing MCP tool to land the fixes the scanner surfaces. A Pro API key from agent-ready.dev is required (issue one from https://agent-ready.dev/dashboard/api-keys).

tools.name: scan_site
tools.title: Scan a site for AI agent readability
tools.description: Runs the agent-ready.dev scanner against a URL and returns structured results: a 0–100 Vercel agent-readability score, a 0–100 llms.txt sub-score, and per-check findings with `howToFix` strings. The 60 checks span four spec families — Vercel Agent Readability Spec (S1–S15 site-wide + P1–P23 per-page), llmstxt.org (L1–L10), and agent protocols (C1–C12 covering MCP, A2A, agents.json, agent-permissions.json, UCP, x402, NLWeb). Use when the user wants a fresh audit of a publicly-reachable URL. Accepts an optional `pageLimit` (1–2000, capped by plan: Free 25, Pro 250, Team 2000). The hosted REST endpoint returns a 202 placeholder immediately and runs the scan asynchronously; this tool polls every 2 seconds for up to 60 seconds and returns the full scan if it completes, otherwise it returns `{ id, status: 'running', pollUrl }` so the caller can poll later with get_scan. Read-only and idempotent — safe to retry. Requires AGENT_READY_API_KEY; without it the tool returns an isError configuration message. Rate limits: Pro 10 scans/minute and 200/day, Team 60/minute and 2000/day.

url.description: Fully-qualified URL to scan, including scheme (https://...). Private and reserved IP ranges are blocked at the network layer.

pageLimit.description: Optional maximum number of pages to crawl from the root URL. Capped by your plan (Free 25, Pro 250, Team 2000).

annotations.title: Scan a site for AI agent readability

tools.name: get_scan
tools.title: Get a previous scan by id
tools.description: Fetches a completed or in-progress scan by its 10-character id. Returns the same scan object shape as scan_site when complete. Returns an isError `not_found` payload when the id is unknown or doesn't belong to the authenticated user's API key. Use after scan_site returns a `running` placeholder, or when the user explicitly references a previous scan by id. Read-only and idempotent — safe to retry. Read rate limit: 120/minute.

id.description: Scan id returned by an earlier scan_site call (10-character nanoid).

annotations.title: Get a previous scan by id

tools.name: ask
tools.title: Ask Agent Ready in natural language
tools.description: Natural-language search (NLWeb /ask) over Agent Ready's own content — scoring methodology, the check registry, and the specs it validates. Public, no API key required. Returns Schema.org-typed result objects (Article / TechArticle / WebPage). Optional `itemType` narrows to a corpus type (methodology, checks, specs, llms-txt, check); `mode: 'summarize'` adds an extractive summary over the top results. Read-only and idempotent.

q.description: Natural-language question about Agent Ready's methodology, checks, or the specs it validates.

itemType.description: Optional filter narrowing the search to one corpus type.

mode.description: 'summarize' adds an extractive summary over the top results.

annotations.title: Ask Agent Ready in natural language

prompts.name: scan
prompts.description: Run a fresh agent-readability scan against a URL and summarise the most important findings.
prompts.text: Scan ${arguments.url} for AI agent readability and summarise the score, rating, and top failing checks.

prompts.name: interpret_scan
prompts.description: Fetch a previous scan by id and translate the per-check findings into plain-English explanations.

arguments.name: id
arguments.description: Scan id returned by an earlier scan_site call.

prompts.text: Fetch scan ${arguments.id} and explain its findings in plain English, grouped by category.

prompts.name: remediation_plan
prompts.description: Fetch a scan by id and produce a prioritised, actionable remediation plan, optionally focused on SEO or agent-protocol fixes.

arguments.name: id
arguments.description: Scan id returned by an earlier scan_site call.

prompts.text: Fetch scan ${arguments.id} and produce a prioritised remediation plan (focus: ${arguments.focus}).

resources.name: methodology
resources.title: Scoring methodology
resources.description: How Agent Ready computes the 0–100 readability score and the llms.txt sub-score. Covers the 60 checks across four categories, rating bands, weighting, and JS-rendering handling.

resources.name: checks
resources.title: Check registry
resources.description: Reference table of all 60 checks Agent Ready runs, grouped by category (site, page, llms.txt, protocol). Each row pairs the stable check ID (e.g. P11, S15, L9, C3) with its human-readable name.

resources.name: llms-txt
resources.title: Agent Ready's own llms.txt
resources.description: The /llms.txt file agent-ready.dev publishes for AI agents discovering it as a tool. Useful as a worked example for the llms.txt validator and for clients introspecting Agent Ready's surface.

resources.name: specs
resources.title: Specifications Agent Ready validates against
resources.description: Canonical URLs and check-ID mappings for the specifications Agent Ready implements: Vercel Agent Readability Spec, llmstxt.org, MCP Server Cards (SEP-1649 / RFC 9728), A2A Agent Cards (a2a.proto v1.0.0), Wildcard agents.json, agent-permissions.json, UCP (RFC 8414), x402 Payment Required, and NLWeb.

api_key.title: Agent Ready API Key
api_key.description: Your Agent Ready Pro API key. Required — the server starts without one but every tool call returns a configuration message until a key is set. Issue a Pro API key at https://agent-ready.dev/dashboard/api-keys.

api_url.title: API base URL (advanced)
api_url.description: Override the Agent Ready API base URL. Leave blank unless you're pointing at a staging or self-hosted deployment.

scan_timeout_ms.title: Scan poll timeout (ms)
scan_timeout_ms.description: How long scan_site polls the hosted API for completion before returning a running placeholder. Default 60000.
